notes:
đè = to press down on something with force, so it can’t move or it gets squashed. It often implies weight or pressure from above.

You can use it for physical pressure (hands, body, heavy objects) and also for control or dominance over someone. It’s often followed by a direct object: something or someone being pressed.

It can also mean “to suppress” or “to hold back” emotions or reactions, like pushing them down inside.

Close synonym: ấn (press), but đè feels heavier and stronger.
exampleSentences: Anh đừng đè mạnh lên chiếc bánh vì nó sẽ bị vỡ.
Khi em đè cuốn sách xuống bàn, nó không còn bị gió thổi bay nữa.
Chú mèo thích đè chân lên tay chị khi ngủ.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Don't press down hard on the cake because it will break.
When I press down the book on the table, it doesn't get blown away anymore.
The cat likes to press down its paws on my hand when sleeping.
notes_md:
**đè** = to press down on something with force, so it can’t move or it gets squashed. It often implies weight or pressure from above.

You can use it for physical pressure (hands, body, heavy objects) and also for control or dominance over someone. It’s often followed by a direct object: something or someone being pressed.

It can also mean “to suppress” or “to hold back” emotions or reactions, like pushing them down inside.

Close synonym: **ấn** (press), but *đè* feels heavier and stronger.

definitions.vi: Động từ chỉ hành động dùng sức nặng hoặc áp lực để giữ chặt, ấn xuống một vật hoặc người khác.
definitions.en: To press down; to squash; to hold back with weight.

example.en: Don't press down on my hand.

content.sentence: Anh đừng ___ mạnh lên chiếc bánh vì nó sẽ bị vỡ.

translation.en: Don't press down hard on the cake because it will break.

content.sentence: Khi em ___ cuốn sách xuống bàn, nó không bị gió thổi bay nữa.

translation.en: When I press down the book on the table, it doesn't get blown away anymore.
